title: Mario Gabelli's Gabelli Asset Fund 2022 Semiannual Report
Permian Oil Rig Count Rises After 3 Straight Weeks of Decline
text: In its weekly release, Baker Hughes Company (BKR) reports that the oil rig count is higher in the Permian Basin.

title: Is Now the Time to Buy Oil Equipment and Services Stocks?
text: There's a healthy bull vs. bear debate over the oil services sector.

title: EOG Resources (EOG) Jumps 76.9% In a Year: More Room to Run?
text: Higher oil price is aiding EOG Resources (EOG).

title: EOG Resources: Thriving In An Energy Crisis
text: The U.S. enjoyed cheaper oil and gas than the rest of the world. Due to underinvestment in the E&P sectors, this could change quickly. The U.S. has three fields that make up a total of 52% of the U.S.'s total gas production. Production is reaching a natural limit and we see declining growth rates.

title: 4 Stocks That Look Like Bargains
text: As of Friday, Aug. 12, the following stocks look undervalued by the market as their price-earnings ratios without non-recurring items trade below 20 while their price-earnings to growth (PEG) ratios trade near or below 1.

title: APA Meets on Q2 Earnings, Buys Assets, Lowers Output View
text: APA Corporation (APA) bought back 7 million shares at $41.59 apiece during the second quarter. The company also spent $43 million in the form of dividend payment.

title: 3 Companies Shelling Out Special Dividends
text: When companies have a spectacular quarter, they sometimes reward shareholders with a special dividend. To income investors, it feels like the cherry on top.

title: EOG Resources Is A Strong Investment On Recent Weakness
text: EOG Resources has a $60 billion market capitalization and a mere $2 billion of net debt showing the strength of its financial position. The company is consistently increasing its base dividend while announcing special dividends to help with returns.

title: Energy ETFs Strengthen on EOG Resources Upbeat Earnings
text: EOG Resources Inc. (NYSE: EOG) shares jumped Friday, lifting energy sector-related exchange traded funds after the oil and gas company beat earnings expectations. On Friday, the iShares U.S. Oil & Gas Exploration & Production ETF (IEO) increased 3.4% and the Energy Select Sector SPDR (XLE) rose 2.6%.

title: EOG Resources, Inc. (EOG) CEO Ezra Yacob on Q2 2022 Results - Earnings Call Transcript
text: EOG Resources, Inc. (NYSE:EOG ) Q2 2022 Earnings Conference Call August 5, 2022 10:00 AM ET Company Participants Tim Driggers – Chief Financial Officer Ezra Yacob – Chief Executive Officer Billy Helms – President and Chief Operating Officer Ken Boedeker – Executive Vice President-Exploration and Production Jeff Leitzell – Executive Vice President-Exploration and Production Lance Terveen – Senior Vice President-Marketing Conference Call Participants Leo Mariani – MKM Partners Arun Jayaram – JPMorgan Chase Scott Hanold – RBC Capital Markets Scott Gruber – Citigroup Neal Dingmann – Truist Securities Doug Leggate – Bank of America Paul Cheng – Scotiabank Jeanine Wai – Barclays Neil Mehta – Goldman Sachs Operator Good day, everyone. And welcome to the EOG Resources' Second Quarter 2022 Earnings Results Conference Call.

title: EOG Resources (EOG) Q2 Earnings Miss Estimates, Revenues Beat
text: Higher lease and well expenses, as well as transportation costs, hurt EOG Resources' (EOG) earnings in Q2.

title: EOG Resources (EOG) Misses Q2 Earnings Estimates
text: EOG Resources (EOG) delivered earnings and revenue surprises of -8.36% and 11.50%, respectively, for the quarter ended June 2022. Do the numbers hold clues to what lies ahead for the stock?

title: Will EOG Resources Stock See Higher Levels Following Its Q2 Results?
text: EOG Resources (NYSE: EOG), an energy company engaging in the exploration, development, production, and marketing of crude oil and natural gas, is scheduled to announce its fiscal second-quarter results on Thursday, August 4.

title: EOG Resources (EOG) to Report Q2 Earnings: What's in Store?
text: Higher oil price is likely to have aided EOG Resources' (EOG) Q2 earnings.

title: Shale Oil Stocks Drop Ahead Of Quarterly Earnings
text: Shale oil producers will be reporting quarterly results this week, coming on the heels of energy giants Exxon Mobil, Chevron and Shell all posting record profits.
